title: Fire breaks out on Rajdhani Express near Ratlam, passengers evacuated
content: A fire broke out in an AC coach of the Thiruvananthapuram-Hazrat Nizamuddin Rajdhani Express in Madhya Pradesh's Ratlam district early Sunday. The B-1 coach, carrying 68 passengers, was detached, and all passengers were safely deboarded without injuries. The cause of the fire is currently under investigation.

title: 20,000-tonne LPG carrier Symi arrives in Gujarat after crossing Hormuz
content: A Marshall Islands-flagged LPG carrier carrying nearly 20,000 tonnes of fuel safely docked in Kandla, having crossed the Strait of Hormuz. This marks a crucial energy supply movement amidst ongoing Middle East conflict, which has disrupted India's crude reserves by nearly 15%.

title: How PBKS vs RCB could decide CSK’s IPL 2026 playoff fate
content: Chennai Super Kings' IPL 2026 playoff hopes hinge on the Punjab Kings vs. Royal Challengers Bengaluru match. An RCB victory would simplify CSK's path to qualification, allowing them to control their destiny by winning their remaining two games. A PBKS win, however, would intensify the pressure and potentially lead to a Net Run Rate battle.

title: 80-year-old kills sleeping wife with axe, then walks to cops in Ghaziabad
content: An 80-year-old man confessed to fatally hacking his 72-year-old wife with an axe while she slept outside their daughter's home. The accused, Harpal, surrendered at a police outpost early Saturday. Authorities found Urmila deceased with multiple axe wounds. Preliminary investigations point to prolonged domestic disputes and the accused's alleged mental health issues as contributing factors to the tragic incident.

title: Amazon, Meta may face 'huge problem' as Americans increasingly oppose data centres in their cities
content: Americans are increasingly opposing the construction of AI data centers in their communities, with seven in ten expressing opposition. Concerns over environmental impact, resource consumption, and local quality of life are driving this sentiment. While tech giants invest billions, local resistance poses a significant hurdle for AI expansion, potentially influencing local elections.

title: Silver import crackdown: Centre tightens rules on white metal — all you need to know
content: India has moved silver imports to a "restricted" category, requiring government approval, to prevent traders from exploiting a duty gap created by a recent 15% import tax hike. This move aims to curb arbitrage opportunities through the India-UAE free trade agreement, protect foreign exchange reserves, and control precious metal inflows, while exempting export-oriented industries.

title: CNG prices hiked again in NCR; 2nd increase in 2 days pushes Delhi rate past Rs 80/kg
content: CNG prices in Delhi-NCR have seen another increase, rising by Re 1 per kg to ₹80.09, following a ₹2 hike just two days prior. This latest revision, effective May 17, mirrors broader fuel price increases across India, attributed to rising global energy costs amid the West Asia conflict.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not a special project for RR: Sangakkara
content: Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old batting sensation, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, is drawing significant fan attention with his aggressive style and impressive strike rate of 236.55.  He has amassed 440 runs and smashed 40 sixes in 11 IPL games. Coaches emphasize letting him enjoy his batting while acknowledging the challenges of evolving against future opposition.

title: Andhra Pradesh announces Rs 30,000 incentive for 3rd child, Rs 40,000 for 4th
content: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Nara Chandrababu Naidu announced financial incentives for families having a third child (₹30,000) and a fourth child (₹40,000) to combat declining population rates. With the state's Total Fertility Rate at a low 1.5, Naidu aims to reverse this trend, viewing children as vital economic resources. This initiative complements existing measures to boost population growth.

title: Dog poisoning led to death of 25 Himalayan griffon vultures in UP
content: Tragic news from Kheri district as 25 Himalayan griffon vultures and two dogs succumbed to insecticide poisoning. Autopsy reports confirm Carbofuran, a potent pesticide, killed the animals. Vultures likely ingested the poison after feeding on the dogs, which had consumed poisoned rice allegedly set out to eliminate them. Authorities are now hunting for those responsible.

title: Behind Arshdeep's struggles: Punjab Kings discover 'restrictions' in pacer's body
content: Punjab Kings bowler Arshdeep Singh struggled this IPL season.  Physical issues and off-field distractions affected his performance.  Team management believes they have found solutions.  Singh is now feeling better physically.  He needs to regain his form to help Punjab Kings.  Leaders like Singh and Iyer must guide the team through this tough phase.

title: Woman abducted, tied to tree, 'assaulted' after rejecting marriage proposal in UP
content: A woman in Uttar Pradesh alleges she was abducted, tied to a tree, and assaulted by a man after rejecting his marriage proposal. The incident occurred on May 11, and police have registered a case and are actively searching for the accused. Further investigation is ongoing following the victim's additional injury disclosures.

title: India’s first 'jihadi drug' seizure: NCB busts Rs 182 crore Captagon racket in Delhi
content: In a groundbreaking operation, India's Narcotics Control Bureau has successfully dismantled an extensive international drug trafficking network, seizing an astonishing 227.7 kg of captagon, a dangerous psychotropic substance often referred to as the 'jihadi drug'.

title: Fuel price hike: Can Centre shield OMCs with Rs 3 cushion, or will rupee flip the script?
content: The government increased fuel prices by Rs 3 per litre to support oil marketing companies facing losses due to high global crude oil prices and a weakening rupee. However, further rupee depreciation could negate these gains, with companies incurring significant daily losses. The price hike offers limited relief, covering only a fraction of projected losses.

title: Explained: What KKR and GT need to reach IPL 2026 playoffs
content: With 10 games left, LSG and MI are out, while GT and RCB are nearing playoff qualification. SRH and RR have strong chances for the top four, with PBKS holding an even shot. CSK's playoff hopes have diminished, and KKR and DC face slim chances.

title: Ebola outbreak: WHO declares international health emergency after rare strain kills 87
content: The World Health Organization declared an international health emergency over a rare Bundibugyo Ebola strain in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, with dozens dead. Cases and deaths are rising, particularly in Congo's Ituri province, where community spread and violence complicate containment efforts. This marks Congo's 17th Ebola outbreak.

title: 'Karuppu' BO day 2: Suriya and Trisha Krishnan's film surges 51%
content: Suriya's 'Karuppu' is setting the box office ablaze, boasting a remarkable 51% increase in earnings on its second day with an impressive Rs 23.40 crore. This gripping tale of a lawyer standing up to a deity has struck a chord with viewers, especially in Tamil Nadu, where ticket sales remain robust.

title: Jasprit Bumrah coming in as captain a fantastic move: Srikkanth
content: Kris Srikkanth lauded Jasprit Bumrah's captaincy in Mumbai Indians' win over Punjab Kings. Bumrah's leadership inspired bowlers like Shardul Thakur and Deepak Chahar, who took crucial wickets. The team displayed a strong will to win, with Tilak Varma's explosive batting sealing the chase. Srikkanth highlighted Bumrah's encouraging presence as a key factor in the bowlers' confidence and performance.

title: Gains of past decades will be lost if crisis continues: PM Modi in The Netherlands
content: Prime Minister Modi warned that global challenges like pandemics, wars, and energy crises could push a large population back into poverty, potentially undoing decades of progress. He highlighted India's "unprecedented" transformation under his leadership, urging the diaspora to join the nation's journey towards becoming a developed country.

title: Woman delivers in phone light in Faridabad hospital park
content: In an unexpected turn of events, a woman gave birth to her child in the park of a government hospital in Faridabad after the main entrance was shuttered for the night, leaving her family to manage the delivery under the glow of their phone's flashlight.

title: 'Athiradi' BO day 3: Tovino Thomas and Vineeth Sreenivasan film crosses Rs 33.89 cr worldwide
content: Tovino Thomas's latest comedy-drama, 'Athiradi', has captivated audiences and smashed box office records, earning a phenomenal ₹33.89 crore globally within its first three days. With standout performances by Basil Joseph and Vineeth Sreenivasan, the film has received glowing reviews, highlighted by ETimes' impressive 4-star rating for its striking visuals and engaging narrative.

title: Chennithala tipped for key role as Satheesan finalises ministry list
content: Kerala's new Congress government is set to take shape. CM-designate V D Satheesan will present the cabinet list to the governor on Sunday. Ramesh Chennithala is expected to secure key portfolios. Several other leaders are also likely to be included. Final decisions on portfolios will be made after discussions with allies.

title: Kolkata survive Gill scare: KKR crack GT code to keep playoff hopes breathing
content: Chasing 248, Gujarat Titans finished on 218 for 4 after Kolkata’s bowlers held their nerve in the death overs.

title: Operation Epic Fury to resume? Trump weighs fresh Iran strikes after China visit: Report
content: As US President Trump returns from China, pressure mounts over Iran. Top aides are reportedly preparing options for renewed military action against Tehran if diplomacy falters, including intensified bombing and potential Special Operations deployments.  Iran, meanwhile, signals readiness for conflict, while the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical concern for global oil prices.

title: UK museum returns 2,000 manuscripts to Jain community in landmark restitution agreement
content: Two thousand Jain manuscripts, collected by British entrepreneur Sir Henry Wellcome and held by the Wellcome Collection in London, are being returned to the Jain community. These 15th to 19th-century texts, believed to be the largest collection outside South Asia, will be transferred to the University of Birmingham.

title: PM Modi Sweden visit LIVE: PM Modi to head to Sweden on third-leg of 5-nation tour after Netherlands
content: PM Modi will hold talks with Swedish counterpart Ulf Kristersson and interact with members of the European Round Table for Industry in Sweden

title: At UN, India flags concerns over targeting of vessels in Strait of Hormuz
content: Mr. Harish's remarks at a special meeting of the United Nations Economic and Social Council (UNECOSOC) on safeguarding energy and supply flows came days after an India-flagged commercial vessel came under attack off the coast of Oman

title: Congress appears locked in last-minute consultations on government formation
content: The politics of factions appeared to play spoilsport, with AICC general secretary in charge of Kerala, Deepadas Munsi, reportedly making haste to prod the faction chiefs into a lasting consensus on cabinet formation before the deadline for submitting the final list of ministers to Kerala Governor Rajendra Arlekar expires at 2 p.m.

title: A summer story
content: Searing summer heat has already caused the electricity demand in Tamil Nadu to touch an all-time high this year, even as power managers struggle to maintain sustained power supply. While extensive infrastructure upgrades and anticipatory power purchase agreements are in place, the reality of a financial crisis in the power sector cannot be wished away, says R. Srikanth

title: INDIA bloc allies want a strong Congress to take on the BJP at the Centre but unwilling to cede space in States
content: Regional parties within the INDIA bloc expect a strong Congress at the Centre to take on the BJP, but want it to remain a marginal player in the States when they are strong

title: Fire breaks out in coach of Rajdhani Express, no casualties
content: The fire broke out in the B-1 coach carrying 68 passengers prompting emergency measures, senior railways commercial manager Sourbah Jain said

title: TVK-led Tamil Nadu government: full list of Ministers
content: The specific portfolios for the nine Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) ministers were formally announced on Saturday (May 16, 2026), with Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay retaining key departments, including the Home and Police.

title: Now book an appointment for bulky waste to be picked up from your doorstep
content: The Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Limited (BSWML), responsible for Solid Waste Management in the city, has launched a new mobile app ‘DClutter’ through which you can book for such a visit by waste collectors

title: Congress expands monitoring committee to oversee Haryana’s SIR exercise; Hooda flays BJP
content: Former Chief Minister and Leader of the Opposition Bhupinder Singh Hooda said the Congress would not allow the wrongful deletion of any legitimate vote or the retention of any invalid vote

title: Congress, activist questions U.P. Culture department’s ‘silence’ over repeated RTI requests on public money
content: Congress leader Alam alleged through delay and even not responding to forwarding by the Chief Secretary office, the culture department is giving impression that it wants to hide information

title: TCS sexual harassment case: Court says clear evidence of abetment by POSH committee member
content: The court had refused bail to TCS site head and POSH committee member Ashwini Chainani, others in the alleged sexual harassment-conversion case

title: President Murmu approves increasing Supreme Court Judge strength from 34 to 38
content: Advocate Sumit Gehlot, a Supreme Court lawyer known for his views on constitutional and judicial issues, said the move was welcome but noted that merely increasing the number of judges would not fully solve the problem of pendency

title: T.N. Cabinet portfolios: CM Vijay to hold Home; Finance for Sengottaiyan; Aadhav Arjuna gets PWD
content: N. Anand has been assigned Rural Development and Water Resources, whereas S. Keerthana has been allocated Industries

title: NEET-UG paper leak: Doctors’ body moves Supreme Court to make NTA a statutory body accountable to Parliament
content: According to United Doctors Front, the 2026 paper leak was part of a “recurring, systemic, and catastrophic failure” of NTA in conducting National Eligibility-cum-Entrance Test”

title: S.P. Velumani | From loyalist to rebel
content: Once seen as Edappadi Palaniswamy’s trusted lieutenant, the AIADMK legislator and former Minister is now leading a revolt within the party following its electoral defeat

title: News in Frames: Symmetry and soul
content: The master chronicler didn’t just document India with his lens; he taught his audience how to see it; his black-and-white statements live on, preserving the soul of a nation

title: Why is the Prime Minister advocating austerity?
content: What are the seven set of suggestions that Prime Minister Narendra Modi made for the public? How has the war in West Asia impacted various sectors in India and how will these cutbacks help? What measures is the government taking?

title: Marathwada floods: Relief out of reach
content: Nearly a year after the Marathwada floods in Maharashtra, many farmers affected by the destruction that followed say they are still waiting for parts of the compensation the government promised. Snehal Mutha finds that some have taken additional loans for the next crop, while some have given farming a break for the year

title: Why has the RSS called for dialogue with Pakistan?
content: What did RSS leader Dattatreya Hosabale say about ties with Pakistan and what reactions did his remarks trigger in India and Pakistan? Can his statement be read as reflecting government policy? What has the RSS’s stance on talks with Pakistan been?

title: Karjat MLA Rohit Pawar hits out at CJI comment on youngsters
content: Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi pulled up a lawyer for “pursuing” a senior advocate designation, saying that there are already “parasites” in society who attack the system, and asked the petitioner whether he wanted to join hands with them

title: Uttarakhand SSC exam cheating racket: Another accused arrested
content: The racket was busted on February 13 this year during the online examination conducted by the SSC for Multi-Tasking (Non-Technical) Staff and Havaldar posts

title: India, Netherlands elevating ties to strategic partnership, says PM Modi
content: India and the Netherlands inked a number of agreements to further enhance their cooperation in a range of areas

title: PM Modi in Netherlands highlights: India, Netherlands elevating ties to strategic partnership, says Modi
content: PM Narendra Modi on May 16 met King of the Netherlands Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima at the Royal Palace and discussed close cooperation in digital technology, innovation, fintech and blue economy

title: Suvendu targets Abhishek, says all his properties will be accounted for
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari held his first public meeting after winning elections at Falta, which falls under the Diamond Harbour Lok Sabha seat held by Abhishek Banerjee; Re-polling in the Falta assembly seat will be held on May 21

title: Centre to roll out pilot programme for linking out-of-school adolescents to open school
content: The initiative will include app-based mapping of out of school children in the age group of 14-18 years, monitoring systems, incentive mechanisms, and district-level convergence strategies

title: BNI meet begins in Jaipur with focus on long-term growth opportunities for startups
content: The two-day conference of the BNI, considered as the world’s largest business networking organisation, seeks to create a platform for building trusted business relationships and fostering new opportunities

title: TMC sends fact-finding teams across Bengal, alleges attacks and killings after poll defeat
content: Party claims over 3,000 workers were attacked and at least 10 killed in post-poll violence; BJP dismisses exercise as “drama” while Calcutta High Court orders safe return of victims

title: India stands at a historic juncture where it possesses a confluence youth power, diversity, scientific temperament, democratic spirit: Karnataka Governor
content: We must ensure that innovation is accessible to every individual in our society. When technology is dedicated to the service of humanity, it truly becomes a catalyst for nation-building

title: India will need 1.5 lakh company secretaries by 2047: ICSI President
content: As of today, the requirement is 10,000 company secretaries, President of The Institute of Company Secretaries of India said at the sidelines of Tamil Nadu State Conference

title: U.P. CM Adityanath lays foundation stone of international cricket stadium in Gorakhpur
content: Speaking about the Tal Nador area, Yogi Adityanath said it was barren and neglected until a few years ago, and that encroachments had since been cleared

title: AIADMK’s rebel camp in no mood to seek early convening of general council
content: A key leader from the camp says: “For the time being, all our focus is on getting the issue of the legislature party resolved at the earliest.”

title: Madras HC grants relief to police personnel who were part of task force formed to catch Veerappan
content: It holds that the appellants participated in risky operations, and says the State govt. should not recover the benefits derived by them during their service as SIs from the date of their promotion

title: Guntur civic chief orders strict vigil against contamination of drinking water
content: Officials told to conduct special audit of drinking water pipelines near the drainage channels and ensure proper documentation of quality test results

title: PM SHRI set to be a tightrope walk for UDF government in Kerala
content: Nearly ₹1,100 crore of Samagra Shiksha funds have been withheld by the Union government since 2023-24; CM-designate V.D. Satheesan had slammed the LDF govt. when it signed on to the scheme in October last year

title: India's aspirations ‘no longer limited to its borders’: Modi to diaspora in Netherlands
content: The Prime Minister, in the second leg of his five-nation tour, meets King and Queen of the Netherlands, addresses Indian diaspora in the European nation

title: Kuki-Zo protestors rally in Manipur, demanding justice for slain church leaders, release of hostages
content: Kuki-Zo groups call for re-imposition of President’s Rule in memo to Home Minister; claiming 14 people are still hostages, Kuki Inpi Manipur chief says “continued captivity is an act of terror against a people”

title: Plan to turn Chinnaganjam beaches into tourism hubs, says Bapatla District Collector
content: Vinod Kumar says says beaches in the mandal will be modelled on Suryalanka and Chirala to boost tourism, revenue generation and local livelihoods

title: GGHs in A.P. told to submit reports on biomedical waste management
content: The instructions come after a dog fled with an amputated limb from the Government General Hospital premises in Vijayawada

title: Probe ordered after dogs flee with amputated limb from government hospital in Vijayawada
content: NTR district Collector Lakshmisha has ordered inquiry into the incident, and directed the GGH authorities to take action against the negligent staff

title: Chola-era Anaimangalam Plates, in possession of Leiden University since 1862, returned to India
content: The plates will be handed over to the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in New Delhi; the ASI, which functions under the Union Ministry of Culture, will decide whether and where the objects will be exhibited in India.

title: Over 1.76 lakh students register for TNEA 2026
content: Over 1.02 lakh among them have completed payment, while 62,863 of them have uploaded their certificates on the portal

title: Solar-powered blinkers installed at 51 identified hotspots in Ranipet to prevent accidents
content: Narrowness of the carriageway, curves with poor visibility, lack of road markings, pothole-ridden stretches, and a lack of better streetlights are found to be reasons for fatal accidents

title: Trump To Launch Big Iran Strikes? His "Calm Before Storm" AI Post Sparks Buzz
content: The image was shared by Trump at a time the fragile April 8 ceasefire remains in place in the Middle East, but tensions over critical marine chokepoint Strait of Hormuz show no signs of abatement.

title: As Customers Seek New Experiences, Do Brand Loyalty Discounts Still Work?
content: Across hospitality, retail, fintech and travel, the message is the same: customer loyalty is shifting from transactions to relationships.

title: Ex-ISRO Scientist's $1 Billion Startup Eyes Maiden Orbital Rocket Launch
content: In eight years after leaving his secure job at ISRO, where he earned Rs 75,000 a month, Chandana, along with his cofounder Naga Bharath Daka, has created a company now valued at Rs 1,500 crore.

title: "I'm Pained...Was Misquoted": Chief Justice On "Cockroaches" Remark
content: "I am pained to read how a section of the media has misquoted my oral observations made during the hearing of a frivolous case yesterday," Chief Justice of India Surya Kant said

title: Who Are Dutch CEOs That Met PM Modi In Netherlands And What They Discussed
content: PM Modi, accompanied by his Dutch counterpart, also witnessed the signing of an agreement between Tata Electronics and ASML to support the semiconductor Fab in Gujarat.

title: PM Modi Sweden Visit LIVE: PM Modi To Visit Sweden Today On Third Leg Of Five-Nation Tour
content: PM Modi in Netherlands LIVE Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is on a five-nation tour from May 15 to 20, during which he visited the United Arab Emirates as part of the first leg of the visit.

title: India, Netherlands Elevate Ties To Strategic Partnership After PM Modi's Visit
content: PM Modi began his two-day visit to The Hague on Friday as part of four-nation trip to Europe that is aimed at ramping up bilateral ties in a range of key areas.

title: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Tehran Prepares "Mechanism" For Hormuz Traffic, Israel Strikes Lebanon
content: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.

title: US-Iran War News Highlights: Israel, Lebanon Extend Ceasfire, Trump Plans More Iran Strikes
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today in a high-stakes summit between the two countries.

title: Netherlands Returns 1,000-Year-Old Chola-Era Copper Plates To India
content: The artefacts, widely referred to in Europe as the 'Leiden Plates', are among the most historically valuable surviving records from the Chola empire. India had been seeking their return since 2012.

title: Rescue Diver Dies In Search Of 4 Italians Who Drowned In Maldives
content: Mohamed Mahudhee, a member of the Maldivian National Defense Force, died of underwater decompression sickness.

title: Average Students, Extraordinary NEET Scores: Curious Case Of The Rajasthan 5
content: Five children from the Biwal family managed to clear NEET at the same time last year. Three members of the family are key accused in the NEET leak.

title: NDTV Exclusive: Inside British Museum Housing Bhojshala's Saraswati Idol
content: In its judgement declaring the Bhojshala complex a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati or Vagdevi, the court also asked the government to consider formal steps for bringing back the historic idol currently housed in the British Museum in London.

title: Amid Centre's 'Save Fuel' Push, Nostalgic Comeback For Tongas In Jammu Town
content: The Tonga tradition had indeed gone out of fashion, but the fuel conservation drive has brought it back to life.

title: Rs 42 Lakh Compensation To Graphic Designer Injured In 2021 Crash In Maharashtra
content: The Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) in Maharashtra's Thane district has awarded a compensation of Rs 41.81 lakh to a 28-year-old graphic designer who suffered 60 per cent permanent disability following a road accident in 2021.

title: Centre Appoints 2 Joint Secretaries, Joint Directors In National Testing Agency
content: The Centre on Saturday appointed two each joint secretaries and joint directors in the National Testing Agency (NTA), which is under the heat over the NEET-UG paper leak.

title: Tata Electronics Teams Up With ASML For Semiconductor Manufacturing In India
content: Electronics manufacturing company Tata Electronics and Dutch semiconductor equipment major ASML have joined hands to enable chip manufacturing in India, a joint statement said on Saturday.

title: Rock story: Andhra find reveals India’s lost link to Antarctica
content: ‘Smoking gun’ evidence was found in AP’s granulite rocks, which showed same age and chemistry as those in East Antarctica J Umamaheswara Rao is an Assistant Editor, at the Times of India-Visakhapatnam. He reports on urban development affairs, civic infrastructure, planning policies, education, health, science, emerging technologies, startups, research, and data-driven stories. He holds postgraduate degrees in journalism & mass communication, business administration, and English, plus several fellowships & short-term courses. Read More

title: CEA proposes sharp rise in fixed monthly power charges for consumers
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. title: Pune teacher due to retire in 7 months arrested in NEET-UG leak case
content: manisha Gurunath Mandhare is a biology teacher at modern college of arts, science and commerce . she was questioned in Delhi and subsequently arrested . her arrest comes a day after the CBI nabbed retired professor P V Kulkarni .

title: PIO Narv Narvekar, manager of Harvard’s $57bn kitty, bows out
content: Nirmal Narvekar is preparing to retire, closing one of the most consequential – and controversial – chapters in modern university finance . he reportedly informed the governing board that he would step down in 2027 after giving the university time to prepare a succession plan . his departure comes as Harvard’s $57 billion kitty sits at the centre of a political and financial storm triggered by the Trump administration’s cuts to federal funding .

title: Was misquoted, I’m proud of our youth, says CJI Surya Kant
content: a section of media misquoting his comments on youth during courtroom proceedings on friday . he clarified that his angst was against those joining the legal profession with bogus degrees and behaving like parasites . the bench was critical of an advocate who was seeking a judicial direction to designate him as senior advocate .
